title: ophioglossum crotalophoroides subsp. typicum
intro: Ophioglossum crotalophoroides subsp. typicum, also known as the adder's tongue fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Ophioglossum crotalophoroides subsp. typicum typically involve drying and grinding the plant into a powder, which can be used in teas, infusions, or applied topically to affected areas.
